name: Validation/validate-op
description: $validate operation conformance tests ported from fhir262 validation/$validation-op. Covers type-level and system-level $validate with valid and invalid Patient resources, Parameters-wrapped and direct bodies, and an empty Parameters edge case. Assertions inspect the returned OperationOutcome for error/fatal issues rather than HTTP status, because servers differ on whether invalid input yields 200+OperationOutcome or 422.

test.name: type-level $validate: valid patient in Parameters wrapper is accepted
test.description: POST /Patient/$validate with a Parameters-wrapped valid Patient must return an OperationOutcome with no error/fatal issues.

operation.description: POST to Patient/$validate with valid patient in Parameters

assert.description: Operation should return a 2xx success status (warningOnly: some servers use 422 for validation failures)
Response body must be an OperationOutcome
Valid resource: OperationOutcome must have no error or fatal issues

test.name: type-level $validate: invalid patient in Parameters wrapper is rejected
test.description: POST /Patient/$validate with a Parameters-wrapped invalid Patient (name as a plain string) must return an OperationOutcome reporting at least one error/fatal issue.

operation.description: POST to Patient/$validate with invalid patient (name: 'John') in Parameters

assert.description: Response body must be an OperationOutcome
Invalid resource: OperationOutcome must contain at least one error or fatal issue
An issue should reference Patient.name (warningOnly: issue expression format varies across servers)

test.name: system-level $validate: valid patient accepted
test.description: POST /$validate (no resource type prefix) with a Parameters-wrapped valid Patient must return an OperationOutcome with no error/fatal issues.

operation.description: POST to $validate (system-level) with valid patient in Parameters

assert.description: Response body must be an OperationOutcome
Valid resource: OperationOutcome must have no error or fatal issues

test.name: system-level $validate: invalid patient rejected
test.description: POST /$validate (no resource type prefix) with a Parameters-wrapped invalid Patient must return an OperationOutcome reporting at least one error/fatal issue.

operation.description: POST to $validate (system-level) with invalid patient in Parameters

assert.description: Response body must be an OperationOutcome
Invalid resource: OperationOutcome must contain at least one error or fatal issue

test.name: type-level $validate: direct resource body (no Parameters wrapper)
test.description: POST /Patient/$validate with a direct Patient body (not wrapped in Parameters) must return an OperationOutcome with no error/fatal issues.

operation.description: POST to Patient/$validate with a direct Patient body

assert.description: Response body must be an OperationOutcome
Valid resource: OperationOutcome must have no error or fatal issues

test.name: type-level $validate: empty Parameters is invalid
test.description: POST /Patient/$validate with an empty Parameters (no resource parameter) is an edge case. Both the OperationOutcome shape and the rejection behavior are warningOnly because server behavior varies.

operation.description: POST to Patient/$validate with empty Parameters

assert.description: Response body should be an OperationOutcome (warningOnly: behavior varies)
Empty input should be invalid: OperationOutcome should contain an error or fatal issue (warningOnly: behavior varies)
